title: Aeginetia pedunculata
taxonomy: Aeginetia pedunculata is a species of flowering plant in the family Orobanchaceae, commonly known as broomrapes. This plant is a type of hemiparasitic plant, which means it obtains some of its nutrients by parasitizing other plants. The classification of Aeginetia pedunculata is as follows: kingdom - Plantae, clade - Angiosperms, clade - Eudicots, clade - Asterids, order - Lamiales, family - Orobanchaceae, genus - Aeginetia, species - A. pedunculata. As a member of the Orobanchaceae family, it is closely related to other broomrape species. Aeginetia pedunculata is native to Asia, specifically in China and India.
